Prompt Caching: From Zero to Complete Mastery
so i have written 3 articles now and your feedback has been truly amazing
in the last article i wrote about LangChain and today we are going to discuss Prompt Caching which is a really important AI concept
i deeply researched this topic so i could break it down for you in a way that actually makes sense
and as always this is not a generic article
this is going to be very useful and beginner friendly like a very very beginner level breakdown
by the end of this article you will have a 90% understanding of prompt caching from scratch
i have also added my own notes throughout the article so you get a better and clearer understanding of each concept
lets get into it:

The Problem Before Prompt Caching

here is what happens without prompt caching
every time you send a message to an AI model like Claude or GPT you are not just sending your question
you are sending a massive block of instructions along with it
the system prompt that tells the AI how to behave the rules and context about your product sometimes entire documents it needs to reference
and the AI has to read and process all of that from scratch every single time
imagine you hired a new employee and every morning you had to re-explain the entire company handbook before they could answer a single question
that is exactly what happens without prompt caching
the AI forgets everything between conversations so your app sends the same 5000 words of instructions with every single request
and you pay for every word every time

Extremely Simple Example (Non-Technical)

think about it like ordering coffee at the same cafe every day
without prompt caching you walk in every morning and say
"hi my name is Ali I like oat milk and I want a medium cup and I prefer light roast and please make it hot not iced"
every single morning the barista listens to the whole thing from scratch
with prompt caching you say that once
the barista remembers it
now every morning you just walk in and say "the usual"
same result and way faster

Real-Life Example

processing
Customer support bot
       ↓
User asks about refund
       ↓
Bot generates answer
       ↓
Next customer asks same question
       ↓
Bot regenerates answer


Inefficient without caching
let me make this even more concrete
say you are building a customer support chatbot for an online store
your system prompt includes
your company policies and return rules shipping timelines and tone of voice instructions a list of 200 frequently asked questions
now imagine 10000 customers message your chatbot today
without prompt caching your app sends that entire block of instructions 10000 times and you pay for it 10000 times
with prompt caching the AI reads it once and remembers it for a while
the next 9999 requests just reference what is already stored
you just saved a massive amount of money and made every response faster

Definition of Prompt Caching

so what actually is prompt caching
prompt caching is when the AI provider stores the static part of your prompt so it does not have to reprocess it every time you send a new request
the "static part" means the stuff that stays the same between requests
your system instructions your rules and guidelines reference documents any context that does not change from user to user
the AI provider keeps that stored and ready to go
so when your next request comes in only the new part (the actual user question) needs to be processed fresh
everything else is already loaded
that is prompt caching in one sentence
store the repeated stuff so you only pay to process it once

How Prompt Caching Works Step by Step

processing
Step 1: Receive prompt
       ↓
Step 2: Check cache for prompt
       ↓                 ↓
     Yes                  No
      ↓                    ↓
Return cached answer ,  Generate answer via AI
                           ↓
                     Store answer in cache
here is how it actually works behind the scenes
step 1: your app sends a request to the AI model with a big system prompt and a user question
step 2: the AI provider looks at the static portion of your prompt and creates a "cached version" of it
step 3: it processes the full request normally and sends back a response
step 4: the next time your app sends a request with that same static prompt the provider recognizes it
step 5: instead of reprocessing that entire static block it loads the cached version instantly
step 6: only the new user message gets processed from scratch
step 7: you get a faster response and you pay a reduced rate for all the cached content
the key thing to understand is that the AI is not skipping your instructions
it is still using them
it just does not have to re-read them from zero every time

Simple Example in Apps

processing
User
 ↓
API Gateway
 ↓
Output Cache (CDN / Redis)
 ↓
Prompt Cache (AI-aware)
 ↓
OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini API
 ↓
Response
let me show you how this plays out in a real app scenario
say you built an AI writing assistant
your system prompt says
"you are a professional copywriter who writes in a casual tone and always uses short sentences and never uses jargon"
that prompt is maybe 500 words long with all the details
user one asks "write me a product description for sneakers"
user two asks "write me an email subject line for a sale"
user three asks "rewrite this paragraph to sound more friendly"
the 500 word system prompt is identical in all three requests
only the user message changes
without caching you process 500 words three times for no reason
with caching you process it once and reuse it for all three

With Prompt Caching

processing
User → Prompt Caching System → Cached Answer? 
          ↓Yes                           ↓No
      Return cached                   Call AI Model
      Answer                          Save result in cache
                                       Return Answer
with prompt caching turned on the difference is night and day
speed improves because the AI does not have to re-read thousands of words of instructions cost drops because cached content is billed at a fraction of the normal rate consistency stays the same because the AI is still following every instruction
some providers charge up to 90% less for cached prompt content
that means if you were spending $1000 a month on AI calls a significant chunk of that could disappear just by enabling caching on the parts of your prompt that never change
your users get quicker answers and your app feels snappier
all from one optimization

Why Companies Use Prompt Caching

the reasons are straightforward
it saves money and at scale the savings are massive it makes responses faster which means better user experience it reduces load on the AI provider meaning fewer timeouts and errors it lets companies use longer more detailed system prompts without worrying about cost
that last point is huge
before prompt caching companies kept their system prompts short because every extra word cost money on every request
now you can write incredibly detailed instructions without the cost multiplying across every user interaction
better instructions mean better AI output and prompt caching makes better instructions affordable

What Is Standard Output Caching

processing
User Request → Server generates full response → Response stored
       ↓
Next identical request
       ↓
Return stored response without recomputing

Note: No understanding of prompt or AI specifics
before we go further you need to understand a different kind of caching that already exists everywhere
standard output caching (or just regular caching) stores the answer itself
if someone asks "what is your return policy" and the AI generates an answer that answer gets saved
the next time someone asks the exact same question the system just serves the saved answer without calling the AI at all
this is how most websites work
you visit a page and the server saves a copy so the next visitor gets it instantly without the server rebuilding it
output caching stores the result
prompt caching stores the instructions
these are very different things

Difference Between Output Caching and Prompt Caching

here is the clearest way to think about it
output caching says "someone already asked this exact question so here is the saved answer"
prompt caching says "the AI already knows the background context so it only needs to process the new question"
output caching skips the AI entirely for repeated questions
prompt caching still uses the AI for every question but makes each call cheaper and faster
output caching only works when the question is identical or nearly identical
prompt caching works on every single request as long as the system prompt stays the same
output caching gives you the same answer every time
prompt caching gives you a fresh answer every time just with lower cost
they solve different problems
and smart companies often use both together

Why Prompt Caching Is Safer Than Output Caching for AI

here is something most people miss
output caching can be dangerous for AI applications
because AI conversations are dynamic
if someone asks "what should I do about my account" the right answer depends on who is asking
if you cache the output you might serve person B the answer that was meant for person A
prompt caching does not have this risk because it never stores or reuses the answer
it only stores the instructions
every response is still generated fresh by the AI based on the specific user input
it just starts from a warm engine instead of a cold one
prompt caching gives you the cost savings without the risk of serving stale or wrong answers

What Exactly Is Stored in the Cache

let me be very specific here
the cache stores a processed version of the static text in your prompt
not the raw text itself but the internal representation the AI created after reading it
think of it like reading a textbook where your brain creates a mental model of the information
the cache is like that mental model so the AI does not have to read it again next time
what gets cached
system prompts and static instructions reference documents you include with every call few-shot examples you provide to guide behavior
what does not get cached
the user message (always new) the AI response (always generated fresh) anything that changes between requests

Simple API-Level Example (Beginner-Friendly)

processing
API receives request
       ↓
Check if "prompt+settings" exists in cache
       ↓                 ↓
Yes → Return cached response       No → Call AI → Save response → Return response
if you are a developer or just curious here is what this looks like in practice
imagine your normal API call looks like this
send: system prompt (2000 words) + user message (50 words) AI processes: 2050 words you pay for: 2050 words
with prompt caching on your first call
send: system prompt (2000 words marked as cacheable) + user message (50 words) AI processes: 2050 words and caches the system prompt you pay for: 2050 words (plus a small one-time caching fee)
every call after that
send: reference to cached system prompt + user message (50 words) AI processes: 50 new words and loads the cached 2000 from memory you pay for: 50 words at full price + 2000 words at the discounted cached rate
that discounted rate can be 90% cheaper depending on the provider
multiply that across thousands of requests per day and the savings are significant

Real SaaS Architecture Example

let me paint the full picture of how a real company would set this up
imagine you are running an AI-powered legal assistant for small businesses
your system prompt includes
3000 words of legal guidelines 1000 words of tone and formatting rules 2000 words of example questions and ideal answers
that is 6000 words of static content sent with every request
you have 5000 users per day each asking an average of 3 questions
that is 15000 requests per day
without prompt caching you process 6000 static words x 15000 requests = 90 million words of repeated content per day
with prompt caching you process those 6000 words once and every subsequent request only processes the new user question
your daily bill drops dramatically and your response times improve across the board
your users never notice any difference in quality because the AI still follows every single instruction just more efficiently

When Prompt Caching Is Perfect

prompt caching works best in very specific situations
when your system prompt is long and detailed when you make many requests with the same base instructions when speed matters and you want faster responses when you include reference documents or knowledge bases in your prompt when your app serves many users with the same underlying AI configuration
the longer your static prompt and the more requests you make the bigger the benefit

Important Limitation (Very Important)

here is where most guides stop but this part matters just as much
prompt caching has real limitations you need to understand
the cache expires after a set period of time (usually minutes not hours) so if your app has low traffic the cache might expire between requests and you lose the benefit
minimum size requirements exist with most providers meaning your static prompt needs to be above a certain length before caching even kicks in
if you change even a small part of your cached content the entire cache gets invalidated and has to be rebuilt from scratch
the cache is not shared across different system prompts so if you have 10 different AI features with 10 different prompts each one needs its own cache
prompt caching does not help with the first request because the cache has to be built on that initial call
ordering matters because the cached content typically needs to be at the beginning of your prompt and anything after the first change breaks the cache
this means you need to design your prompts carefully
put the stable stuff first
put the changing stuff last
keep your static content truly static
and make sure you have enough request volume to actually benefit from the cache before it expires
if you get the architecture right the savings and speed improvements are massive
if you get it wrong you might pay extra caching fees without getting any benefit

Bringing It All Together

prompt caching is one of those concepts that sounds complicated but is actually beautifully simple once you see the full picture
the AI re-reads the same instructions on every request which wastes time and money
prompt caching stores those instructions so the AI only reads them once
it is different from output caching which stores answers because prompt caching stores the question setup not the answer
this means every response is still fresh and personalized and accurate
companies use it to cut costs by up to 90% on repeated prompt content while making their apps faster and their AI instructions more detailed
the cache stores the processed version of your static prompt and it only works when that content stays exactly the same between requests
it is perfect for chatbots and AI assistants and any app making many calls with the same base instructions
but it has real limitations around expiration times and minimum sizes and the need for careful prompt ordering
if you understand all of that you now know more about prompt caching than most developers building with AI today
and you did not need a computer science degree to get there
